entities.name: SUBMERGED SOILS
entities.description: Soils saturated with water, leading to anaerobic conditions and a reduced state

entities.name: ARABLE SOILS
entities.description: Soils suitable for farming, typically well-aerated and contrasting with submerged soils

entities.name: LACK OF OXYGEN
entities.description: The anaerobic state resulting from soil submergence that drives chemical and biological changes

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth

entities.name: WETLAND RICE
entities.description: A crop variety adapted for growth in submerged soil environments

entities.name: NITROGEN MINERALIZATION
entities.description: The biological process of converting organic nitrogen into inorganic forms

entities.name: AMMONIUM PRODUCTION
entities.description: The end point of the nitrogen mineralization process in anaerobic submerged soils

entities.name: NITRIFICATION
entities.description: The biological oxidation of ammonium to nitrate, which is inhibited in submerged soils

entities.name: IRON
entities.description: A chemical element abundant in tropical rice soils that undergoes redox changes

entities.name: REDOX CYCLING OF IRON
entities.description: The process of iron switching between oxidation states, influencing soil biogeochemistry

entities.name: BIOGEOCHEMISTRY
entities.description: The chemical, physical, geological, and biological processes governing the composition of the environment

entities.name: AQUATIC SEDIMENTS
entities.description: Deposits of organic and inorganic matter at the bottom of water bodies

entities.name: REDUCIBLE IRON
entities.description: Iron species that can be reduced during redox reactions in submerged environments

entities.name: REDOX REACTIONS
entities.description: Chemical reactions involving the transfer of electrons between species

entities.name: TROPICS
entities.description: Geographic regions characterized by high temperatures where rice soils are often rich in iron
